title_en: The System for Addressing Gambling Harm Is Broken
title_zh: 应对赌博危害的体系已经失灵
rubric_en: Readers respond to our April 2026 cover story and more.
rubric_zh: 读者对我们2026年4月封面故事及更多内容的回应

paragraphs.en: In the April issue, McKay Coppins reflected on his year as a degenerate gambler.
paragraphs.zh: 在4月刊中，麦凯·康皮斯回顾了他作为一个严重赌徒度过的一年。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps the most sinister aspect of McKay Coppins’s well-researched story on sports gambling is the combination of addictions cultivated by the app-based platforms he used. In the apps, the pathology of compulsive gambling partners with a ubiquitous pathology we all suffer from now: an addiction to our phones. One cannot help but wonder if, 50 years from now, we’ll look back on our current norms with the same disbelief we today express when discussing cigarette smoking during its heyday. Online gambling comes with its own set of life-altering problems, to be sure, but it exists in the context of a serious addiction—screen time—that now afflicts everyone, not just gamblers.
paragraphs.zh: 麦凯·康皮斯这篇关于体育投注的深度调查里，最阴险之处之一，是他使用的应用平台培养出的成瘾组合。在这些应用中，强迫性赌博的病态，与我们如今几乎人人都在遭受的一种普遍病态结合在了一起：对手机的依赖。人们不禁会想，五十年后回望今日的生活方式，我们会不会像今天提及香烟鼎盛时期那样惊讶。无可否认，网络赌博会带来一系列改变人生的问题，但它发生在一个更严重的成瘾背景之中——屏幕时间成瘾——这种问题如今笼罩着每个人，不只是赌徒。

paragraphs.en: Tom Langston, M.D. Lookout Mountain, Ga.
paragraphs.zh: 汤姆·兰克斯顿，医学博士，乔治亚州洛基特山

paragraphs.en: McKay Coppins criticizes Justice Samuel Alito for ignoring the “public-policy rationale” behind the federal sports-betting ban. But that was exactly Alito’s job as a judge. The question in Murphy v. NCAA was whether Congress could constitutionally order state legislatures to maintain state laws prohibiting sports betting. Under the anti-commandeering doctrine, it cannot. This is the same constitutional safeguard that would prevent a future Congress from, say, requiring states to keep their abortion bans on the books.
paragraphs.zh: 麦凯·康皮斯批评塞缪尔·阿利托大法官忽视了联邦体育投注禁令背后的“公共政策依据”。但这正是阿利托作为法官本该履行的职责。争议在于《墨菲诉全国大学体育协会》一案中，国会是否有宪法依据命令各州维持禁止体育投注的州法。依据“反征用学说”，它不能。正是这一同样的宪法保障，未来将会阻止国会要求各州继续保留其堕胎禁令。

paragraphs.en: As Coppins notes, experts estimate that only 2 to 5 percent of gamblers develop compulsive behaviors. That is a serious concern for those individuals. But when else do we ban an activity enjoyed responsibly by the majority because a small minority struggles? We tried that with alcohol and abandoned it. The consistent position on personal liberty must be that adults can make their own choices. Targeted regulation of predatory marketing and better self-exclusion tools are reasonable. Treating every adult sports bettor as a rube who needs saving is not.
paragraphs.zh: 正如科平斯（Coppins）所指出的，专家估计只有约2%到5%的赌徒会发展为强迫性赌博行为。对这些人来说，这是一个严肃的问题。但我们还会因为少数人受困而禁止多数人负责任地享受的活动吗？我们在酒类问题上已经尝试过这种做法，后来也放弃了。关于个人自由，一贯的立场应是成年人有权做自己的选择。对掠夺性营销进行有针对性的监管，以及提供更完善的自我排除工具，都是合理的做法。把每一位成年体育投注者都当成需要被拯救的傻瓜并不合理。

paragraphs.en: Owen Voutsinas-Klose Washington, D.C.
paragraphs.zh: Owen Voutsinas-Klose Washington, D.C.

paragraphs.en: After reading McKay Coppins’s article, I keep thinking about how easy it would be for him to start gambling again. I wonder: Has he ever crossed from Virginia into any of the five bordering states that wouldn’t honor his self-exclusion in order to make a bet? Has he made a sports-related wager on one of the prediction markets available in all 50 states? Has he received offers for free bets in his inbox, even after opting out?
paragraphs.zh: 在读了麦凯·科平斯（McKay Coppins）的文章后，我一直在想他要重新开始赌博会有多容易。我在想：他是否曾经跨过弗吉尼亚州，去了五个毗邻州中任何一个不承认他自我排除状态的地方来下注？他是否在任何一个覆盖50个州的预测市场上进行过体育赛事投注？即使他选择了退出，是否仍会在邮箱里收到免费投注的邀请？

paragraphs.en: I know this cycle firsthand. I’m one of the millions of Americans in recovery for a gambling addiction. To borrow Coppins’s words, I am a fellow degenerate. I knew I had a problem as a teenager playing poker in friends’ basements, and it escalated through my 20s in casinos and on online sportsbooks.
paragraphs.zh: 我亲身经历过这个循环。我是成千上万正在戒赌的美国人中的一位。借用科平斯的话，我是同样“有问题”的一类人。我在十几岁时就知道自己在朋友地下室打扑克时已经有问题，到了20多岁又在赌场和网络体育投注中不断加重。

paragraphs.en: I tried to stop myself more than 100 times. Therapy, Gamblers Anonymous, new cities, new social circles—nothing worked; the hook was set in my brain. I finally found recovery after more than a decade. I had lost hundreds of thousands of dollars, and I’d nearly lost my life. Today, six years bet-free, I manage it like a chronic disease. I have also founded Evive, a digital health company focused on supporting people with gambling problems.
paragraphs.zh: 我曾尝试戒掉赌博一百多次。心理治疗、匿名戒赌者协会、换新城市、换社交圈——都没能奏效；我的大脑像被上了钩一样。我最终在十多年后找到了康复之路。我当时损失了几十万美元，几乎也把自己的生命都丢了。如今已经戒赌六年了，我把它当作一种慢性病来管理。我还创办了 Evive，这是一家以帮助有赌博问题的人为目标的数字健康公司。

paragraphs.en: Self-exclusion, which Coppins pursued at the end of his reporting, is a first step. I use it too. But it is a screen-door barrier, not a solution. What actually works is what you build on the other side of that door: accountability tools, a community of people who get what you’re going through, and a better understanding of what’s happening in your brain.
paragraphs.zh: Coppins 在报道结尾谈到的“自我排除”是第一步。我也在用它。但它更像一扇筛网式的隔离门，而不是解决方案。真正有效的是你在这扇门之外所建立的东西：可持续的责任追踪工具、一个能理解你正在经历的一切的社群，以及你更深入的自我认知，明白大脑里到底发生了什么。

paragraphs.en: The system meant to address gambling harm is underfunded, underutilized, and built for an era before gambling was a 24/7 digital activity. There’s no shortage of infrastructure designed to bring you back into gambling, and almost none designed to help you stay out.
paragraphs.zh: 用于应对赌博伤害的体系如今经费不足、利用率低，而且是为赌博还没成为24/7数字化活动的时代而设计的。为了让你回到赌博，几乎不缺乏相关基础设施；而真正帮你远离赌博的设施却几乎没有。

paragraphs.en: Coppins has shown what gambling harm looks like today: It can hit anyone. His article invites a reckoning with what support looks like. The groundwork is there: digital tools that reach people before they’ve hit rock bottom, peer support. We need casino operators, public-health agencies, regulators, and technological innovators to work together to create solutions as layered as the problem. We all deserve something more durable than a screen door.
paragraphs.zh: Coppins 展现了当下赌博伤害的样貌：它可以伤及任何人。他的文章呼吁我们重新审视“支持”意味着什么。基础已经具备：能在你还没跌入谷底前就触达人的数字化工具、同伴支持。我们需要赌场运营方、公共卫生机构、监管者以及技术创新者共同合作，打造与问题同样“分层”的解决方案。我们都应获得比一扇筛网门更持久、更有力的帮助。

paragraphs.en: Sam DeMello Beverly, Mass.
paragraphs.zh: 萨姆·德梅洛 摄，美国马萨诸塞州贝弗利

paragraphs.en: The Women of Avenger Field
paragraphs.zh: 阿文杰空军基地的女性

paragraphs.en: They bravely served as pilots during World War II, Ellen Cushing wrote in the April issue. Then America forgot them.
paragraphs.zh: 伊伦·卡辛在4月刊写道：他们在二战期间勇敢地担任了飞行员。后来，美国把她们给忘了。

paragraphs.en: In 1943 my mother, 25 and single, left her job with IBM to join the war effort. She held a commercial pilot’s license, so she joined the Women Airforce Service Pilots and reported for training. On orders from General Hap Arnold, she and 25 other WASPs were then transferred to Camp Davis, in North Carolina.
paragraphs.zh: 1943年，我母亲25岁且未婚，辞去了在IBM的工作，参加了战争努力。她持有商业飞行员执照，因此加入了女性空军服务飞行员小组（WASP）并前往训练。根据哈普·阿诺德将军的命令，她和另外25名WASP随后被调往北卡罗来纳州的戴维斯营。

paragraphs.en: My mom’s cohort was assigned to tow targets behind their aircraft so gunners could practice tracking and firing 90-mm and 40-mm shells. Many of the planes returned with holes; the pilots returned with shaken nerves.
paragraphs.zh: 我母亲所在的编队负责在飞机后方拖拽靶标，让炮手练习跟踪并发射90毫米和40毫米炮弹。许多飞机都回到基地时带着弹孔；飞行员则带着受惊的神经回来了。

paragraphs.en: The period was marked by several crashes and overt hostility from the base commander. Mom was proud of her service, but described it to my siblings and me as the most frightening time of her life. She was grounded with an ear infection in 1944 and left the WASPs, transferring to the Office of Strategic Services. Mom died in 2004, having finally been recognized for her service as a veteran. At her burial in Fremont, New Hampshire, she was attended by a military honor guard. A few years later, she received the Congressional Gold Medal. Her name was Frederica McAfee Richardson.
paragraphs.zh: 那段时期充满了多起坠机事件，以及基地指挥官公开的敌意。母亲为自己的服役感到骄傲，但她向我和我的兄弟姐妹描述时说，那是她人生中最惊恐的时期。1944年，她因耳部感染而被迫停飞，并离开了WASP，转入战略服务局。她于2004年去世，终于被正式承认是退伍军人。她在新罕布什尔州弗里蒙特的葬礼上由军队荣誉仪仗队列队致敬。几年后，她获得了国会金质奖章。她的名字是Frederica McAfee Richardson。

paragraphs.en: Steven M. Richardson Winona, Minn.
paragraphs.zh: 史蒂文·M·理查森，明尼苏达州威诺纳

paragraphs.en: My sister, Phyllis, was always more than a little irritated when someone expressed surprise that she, having surpassed 90, was still driving. “Of course I’m still driving,” she would reply. “I flew a B-17.”
paragraphs.zh: 我的姐姐菲利斯每当有人惊讶于她已年过90岁却还在开车时，总是有点不满地回应。“当然我还在开车，”她会说，“我曾驾驶过B-17。”

paragraphs.en: Phyllis was a WASP. Ellen Cushing’s article on the Women Airforce Service Pilots reminded me of all the photographs my grown-up sister sent home from the base: of the “girls” gathered together in the bay, of her climbing into a plane with her leather jacket and parachute. I first viewed those photos when I was 5 years old; they constitute my chief memory of the war.
paragraphs.zh: 菲莉斯是 WASP 成员。艾伦·坎辛的《女性空军服务飞行员》一文让我想起姐姐在部队基地寄回家里的照片：那些“女孩”聚在机库里的合影、她穿着皮夹克背着降落伞爬上飞机的样子。我第一次看到这些照片时只有5岁；它们构成了我关于那场战争的最主要记忆。

paragraphs.en: Phyllis married another pilot in 1945 and raised three children. She was politically conservative and eschewed a feminist label, but she enthusiastically joined other WASPs in a visit to their Russian counterparts in the early days of glasnost, and she was thrilled to attend the ceremony in 2010 in Washington, D.C., where the WASPs were officially, finally recognized and awarded the Congressional Gold Medal. She proudly displayed her medal in her home. But close to it sat her wings, the pin that symbolized what she personally had done for her country. I suspect that this was more important to her.
paragraphs.zh: 菲莉斯于1945年嫁给另一位飞行员，并抚养了三个孩子。她政治立场保守，并不认可“女权主义者”这一称呼，但她热情参加过早期“玻璃诺斯特”时期 WASP 人员对俄罗斯同行的访问，也很激动地出席了2010年在华盛顿特区举行的仪式，在那里 WASP 终于被正式认可并授予国会金质奖章。她把这枚奖章骄傲地展现在家中，但在奖章旁边，放着她的飞行翼徽章，那枚象征她本人为国家所做贡献的徽章。我猜想，对她来说那枚徽章更有分量。

paragraphs.en: Phyllis died in 2018, just short of her 100th birthday; she is buried in a family cemetery on a small island off the coast of Maine. There, etched on her memorial stone, is Fifinella, the winged and goggle-wearing female gremlin depicted on the WASP insignia. Fifinella was designed by Walt Disney for a film based on Roald Dahl’s The Gremlins. (It was never produced.) As gremlins came to be associated with aviation culture—pilots would blame mechanical problems on the pesky creatures—the WASPs adopted Fifinella as their symbol. She was a powerful figure who could wreak havoc on those who did not take her seriously.
paragraphs.zh: 菲莉斯于2018年去世，享年不到100岁；她被安葬在缅因州一座沿海小岛上的家族墓地。她的纪念碑上刻有 Fifinella——一个戴着护目镜、长有翅膀的女性小妖精形象，出自 WASP 的徽标。Fifinella 最初由华特·迪士尼为改编自罗尔德·达尔《小妖精》一书的电影设计（那部电影最终并未拍摄）。后来，当妖精一词逐渐与航空文化联系在一起，飞行员们把机械故障都归咎于这些讨厌的小生物时，WASP 就把 Fifinella 作为了自己的象征。她是个强势的人物，会让那些不认真对待她的人陷入混乱。

paragraphs.en: As the secretary of defense denies promotions to women in the military, as the Trump administration removes webpages telling the stories of the WASPs and other women who served their country, I think of Fifinella. Long may her spirit, and that of all the women who wore the insignia, live on.
paragraphs.zh: 当国防部长继续拒绝晋升军队中的女性时，当特朗普政府下架讲述 WASP 以及其他为国服役女性故事的网页时，我就会想到 Fifinella。愿她的精神，也愿所有佩戴过那枚徽标的女性精神，长存。

paragraphs.en: Christina Marsden Gillis Berkeley, Calif.
paragraphs.zh: 克里斯蒂娜·马斯登·吉利斯，加州伯克利

paragraphs.en: In this month’s cover story, “The Age of Reading Is Over,” Rose Horowitch describes the new postliterate era. The problem is less a crisis of literacy—though many Americans do struggle with basic reading skills—and more a crisis of comprehension. Americans lack the desire to read and are losing the higher-order intellectual powers that come with reading extended texts. For the cover, we used transparent paper to make the headline appear as if it’s fading into obscurity.
paragraphs.zh: 在本月的封面故事“阅读时代终结”中，罗斯·霍洛伊奇描述了一个新的“后识字”时代。问题并不完全是识字危机——尽管许多美国人确实连基本阅读能力都很吃力——更大问题是理解能力危机。美国人缺乏阅读的欲望，并且正在失去阅读长篇文本所带来的高阶思维能力。为这期封面，我们使用了透明纸张，让标题看起来仿佛逐渐消失进尘封的过去。

paragraphs.en: — Colin Hunter, Art Director
paragraphs.zh: —— 科林·亨特，艺术总监

paragraphs.en: “How to Tell the American Story” (July) described an August car race in Washington, D.C., as a Formula 1 race. In fact, it is an IndyCar race.
paragraphs.zh: “如何讲述美国故事”（七月刊）把华盛顿特区八月的一场汽车赛事描述成了“一级方程式”比赛。实际上，那是一场印第卡车赛。

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the August 2026 print edition with the headline “The Commons.”
paragraphs.zh: 本文刊登在2026年8月纸质版中，标题为“The Commons”。
